title: Exact Finite Dual Space Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite dual-space covector additivity QF_UF/Alethe regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Covector additivity conflict where 10 + 01 = 11 and 1 + 1 = 0, but the malformed row forces f(11) = 1 and additivity.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional finite dual-space rows only when they add distinct pairing, annihilator, or transpose-map solver pressure; keep bidual, adjoint, Hahn-Banach, and topological-dual theorems in the Lean-horizon lane.

graduation_criteria: Encode fixed finite dual-space, pairing, annihilator, and transpose-map rows as deterministic finite-function obligations.
Replay covector linearity, pointwise dual operations, dual-basis pairings, annihilators, and transpose maps through Axeyum model evaluation.
Route bad finite covector-additivity conflicts through checked QF_UF/Alethe evidence once exact evaluation replay identifies the failing pair.
Keep general duality, bidual theorems, adjoints, topological duals, Hahn-Banach, and functional analysis under Lean horizon until formal routes exist.
